texto: How much have we not seen or felt or heard because there was no word for it -- at least no word we knew? We speak to navigate ourselves away from dark corners and we become, each one of us, cartographers.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Kei Miller que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “How much have we not seen or felt or heard because there was no word for it -- at least no word w…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, cognition, language, com foco em reflexao-noturna e language.

texto: To know a man properly, you must know the shape of his hurt - the specific wound around which his person has been formed like a scab.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kei Miller: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a sabedoria. texto: And now everybody get the understanding that Gilzene did get. A man cannot rise on his own. Not even a powerful man like Alexander Bedward. Him did need us. Him did need Augustown. It was we who had to push him up into the sky. It was we who had to show our faith to the governor and everybody else.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kei Miller: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “And now everybody get the understanding that Gilzene did get. texto: After all, don't care how you want to sit there and deny the knowledge of River Mumma sitting on her rock - don't care how you deny the knowledge of fallen angels who can jump into your body as they please, or the knowledge of ancestors who sit beside your bed and watch when they not harkening on to the sounds of drumming - don't care how you deny any of it, all of it is still true. All of them things still exist, because them do not need the permission of your belief.
explicacao: Citação de Kei Miller sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “After all, don't care how you want to sit there and deny the knowledge of River Mumma sitting on …” articula reflexao, angels, belief em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como belief e crazy.

texto: a hymn then not to birds but to words which themselves feel like feather and wing and light, as if it were on the delicacy of such sweet syllables that flocks take flight.

texto: Maybe you shake your head, but let me learn a lesson right now: plenty knowledge is in this world. Enough knowledge that you can pick and refuse. And if you want, you can refuse to know plenty things, don't care how true those things be. I know things you does not know, and things you will never know. And it is sake of that - sake of this knowledge - that people have looked on me and called me old fool or crazy. They treat me like I is retarded. Imagine that. I is the idiot because I know what they don't know.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kei Miller: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Maybe you shake your head, but let me learn a lesson right now: plenty knowledge is in this world…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, belief, crazy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de crazy e seeing.
